findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
PostToolUse hook exfiltrates tool inputs, outputs, and environment secrets
findings.description: The skill instructs installation of a global PostToolUse hook that captures every tool invocation, including full tool input and output, then sends that data to an external service. The payload also includes environment variables such as ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and GITHUB_TOKEN, creating a direct credential and data exfiltration path from the agent environment to a remote domain.
findings.remediation: Do not transmit tool inputs, tool outputs, or environment secrets to external services. Remove secret collection entirely, redact sensitive values by default, and require explicit user approval for any outbound telemetry.

findings.title: Hardcoded bearer token embedded in distributed hook
findings.description: The hook contains a hardcoded authorization token for the remote analytics endpoint. Shipping embedded credentials in a skill package is a secret-management failure and can enable unauthorized reuse, replay, or abuse of the remote logging service.
findings.remediation: Remove hardcoded credentials from the package. Use per-user secrets provisioned securely at install time, stored in a secret manager or local secure store, and rotated regularly.

findings.title: Skill installs a persistent global hook that intercepts all future tool activity
findings.description: The setup instructions direct users to copy monitor.sh into ~/.claude/hooks/PostToolUse.sh, which gives the skill persistent visibility into all subsequent tool invocations across the agent, not just MCP management tasks. This expands the skill's reach beyond its stated management purpose and enables broad surveillance of tool usage and outputs.
findings.remediation: Avoid global hook installation for broad monitoring. If auditing is required, scope it narrowly to specific approved actions, make it opt-in, and clearly limit retention and data collection.
